notes: Functions fine for the most part after 8 hours of playtime. Performance seems a bit low for my specs and I get random crashes (access violations), although I'm led to believe Windows users suffer from these issues as well. Audio and controller work fine, however I've noticed that during bad weather lightning will flash green. Overall, is playable out of the box, without the need for PROTON_USE_WINED3D.
title: My Time At Portia

notes: I can launch the game without using "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%", however all text are colored squares, and the in-game rendering is terrible, npcs and some furniture does not render at all, and outside some of the map appears with or without the map open, no visible terrain, except for what looks like water everywhere. The machines don't render, either. But with "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%" on, I get no bugs, only some lag.
title: My Time At Portia

notes: Runs out of box without any tweaks, hence marking as Platinum. Performance is not so good with this game compared to what i get from other games like Hitman 2 and The Witcher 3 on same hardware.

notes:
Game runs perfectly for me. Only issue I have is the breaking controls after tabbing out of the game. Keyboard controls aren't working at all and when using a controller only a few buttons are working.
Enabling Wine D3D11 just lowered my fps drastically, but didn't change anything else. With default settings I ran into 60fps limit with max settings.

notes: As long as you can endure the occasional lag spike, the game runs really well. All the D3D11 tweak did for me was tank my frame rate (15-25 FPS in outside areas), so I reverted to standard where I'm hovering around 55-60 FPS. Totally playable.
title: My Time At Portia

notes:
Works mostly perfectly, one setting needed to launch. Just add: "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%" to Launch Options. Then no problems, runs perfectly. Also didn't have any crashes at all even on longer Play Sessions. 

One thing to note, on Stock Cooler for Ryzen 2600 the Temperature is very high at 80-90 Degrees Celsius.
title: My Time At Portia
